top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 82, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
GFNY Florida Sebring's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic GFNY Florida Sebring's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of GFNY Florida Sebring. Train it until it feels boring.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 82 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what GFNY Florida Sebring's mixed terrain actually does to you.
GFNY Florida Sebring's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 82 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 82 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Tempo is GFNY Florida Sebring's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 82 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
At GFNY Florida Sebring, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 82 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for GFNY Florida Sebring’s 82 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and VO2max to prepare you for 82 miles of mixed terrain in Sebring, Florida, USA.
